papers.title_en: HySpecPro: Scalable Hypergraph Partitioning via Spectral Projection Optimization
papers.authors: Rongjian Liang, Zhuo Feng, Haoxing Ren
papers.evidence: 面向3D集成电路设计的VLSI超图划分
papers.tldr: 现代VLSI设计超图分割中，多级方法的粗化阶段可能扭曲结构信息，尤其对高密度超边效果不佳。HySpecPro提出单级谱投影优化方案，通过二分拉普拉斯嵌入和高效投影搜索，结合GPU加速进行端到端优化。实验表明其切割质量与先进多级方法相当，且计算时间随总超边度线性扩展。该方法有效提升了超图分割的可扩展性。
papers.abstract_en: Modern VLSI designs comprise tens of billions of components, making scalable hypergraph partitioning critical for parallel and hierarchical optimization. Although multilevel partitioning remains the dominant paradigm, its coarsening stage can distort structural information, especially in hypergraphs with many high-degree hyperedges, leading to increased refinement overhead and limited scalability. Recent approaches incorporate spectral information to guide coarsening, but only in a heuristic manner, without directly optimizing the partitioning objectives. We introduce HySpecPro, a single-level hypergraph partitioner that performs end-to-end optimization in a spectral embedding space. HySpecPro constructs embeddings from a bipartite Laplacian and performs efficient projection-based search, supported by a fully GPU-accelerated implementation. Experiments show that HySpecPro delivers cut quality comparable to state-of-the-art multilevel methods while scaling linearly with the total hyperedge degree.
